title: Mildly Sour Chinese Tofu Xiaolongbao
description: A traditional Chaozhou dish featuring silken tofu and sour flavors, perfect for a sick day, this recipe is ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly and formed into delicate xiaolongbao.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, soy sauce, grated ginger, and minced garlic to make the sour sauce.
Add the crumbled silken tofu to the bowl and gently mix until the tofu is well coated with the sour sauce.
Stir in chopped scallions and sesame oil.
Lay a sheet of papillote paper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the tofu mixture in the center of the paper.
Dampen the edges of the papillote paper with water and fold the paper into a triangle, pressing the edges to seal the xiaolongbao.
Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients to form 12 xiaolongbao.
Place the xiaolongbao in a steamer lined with parchment paper, leaving some space between each xiaolongbao.
Steam the xiaolongbao over boiling water for 10 minutes.
Serve the xiaolongbao hot with additional sour sauce for dipping if desired.
